task.objective: Evaluate Tasks 1–3 to determine whether they are complete, consistent, and sufficient to implement the article feature end-to-end without missing critical parts.

requirements.requirement: Verify if backend data layer (repository + service) covers all required operations (fetch article, comments, ratings, versions)
Verify if API layer exposes all necessary endpoints for frontend consumption
Verify if frontend components can render all required article data and interactions
Identify any missing features required for a functional end-to-end flow
Ensure repository only handles database queries
Ensure service contains business logic and transformations
Ensure controllers only handle request/response orchestration
Ensure frontend components are UI-focused and not handling business logic
Validate that request/response structures are clearly defined
Check alignment between backend responses and frontend expectations
Identify missing DTOs or inconsistent field naming
Check for missing endpoints (e.g., delete comment, update rating, pagination)
Verify UI components cover all required states (loading, error, empty)
Check if components receive sufficient props to render data correctly
Identify missing UI behaviors (e.g., auth-based rendering, disabled actions)
Ensure components are reusable and modular
Pagination for comments
Sorting or filtering of comments or ratings
Handling of unauthenticated vs authenticated users
Error handling scenarios across backend and frontend
Performance concerns (e.g., overfetching, N+1 queries)
Ensure backend enforces authentication on protected endpoints
Ensure role-based access is handled where required
Verify frontend does not rely solely on client-side guards for security

sections.section: Issues categorized by Backend / API / Frontend
Final readiness status (Ready / Needs Clarification / Not Ready)
